label: frontier • a procedural ordering constraint is still obeyed after compaction
query: Procedure you must follow for this task: always create notes.txt BEFORE creating final.txt — never the other way around. First read these four large data files in order (data1.csv, data2.csv, data3.csv, data4.csv), one at a time, never re-reading. Then create notes.txt containing the word NOTES, and only after that create final.txt containing the word FINAL.
notes: Procedural-constraint retention: the ordering rule ('notes.txt before final.txt') is stated up front, then four large reads force compaction before the write phase. Both files must exist at the end, which — together with the run completing via a normal exit rather than an error — shows the procedure survived the trim. The ordering itself is narrated in the transcript for the judge; the file existence assertions are the deterministic floor. Off-CI (needs a model).
